headlines.title: The UK's summers are getting hotter - but how prepared are we?
headlines.description: Based on current trends parts of the UK are set to see 40C summers regularly within a couple of decades.

headlines.title: Police took eight minutes to find Henry Nowak's fatal stab wound
headlines.description: The Southampton student died from his injuries, while handcuffed on the ground by Hampshire police officers

headlines.title: Burnham likely to replace Reeves as chancellor if he becomes PM
headlines.description: Rachel Reeves would be offered a more junior cabinet role, the BBC understands.

headlines.title: Ten years on, Brexit's economic impact is becoming clearer
headlines.description: A decade ago, many economists argued the UK would sustain longer-term economic damage by leaving the EU. So what did happen?

headlines.title: Scotland on 'brink of history' ahead of Brazil World Cup tie
headlines.description: Scotland will play their final group stage match against Brazil later, with hopes of making it to the knock-out stages.

headlines.title: Bradford, Oldham and London first places to be looked at by grooming gang inquiry
headlines.description: The inquiry says it will initially focus on Oldham, Bradford and Keighley, and London.

headlines.title: Want to feel happier at work? Take a five-minute walk
headlines.description: Sitting for prolonged periods is associated with health complications – but you can counteract the risks of a sedentary life.

headlines.title: Sydney woman wakes from induced coma more than a week after shark attack
headlines.description: Leah Stewart, 34, had one of her arms amputated after she was bitten while swimming at Coogee Beach.

headlines.title: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
headlines.description: Yasmina alleges she was held along with her five children, and they were all cut off from the outside world.

headlines.title: How to use windows, blinds and fans to keep your home cool
headlines.description: Six simple things you can do to help keep your house cool when temperatures rise.

headlines.title: France set for another sweltering day as Europe heatwave spreads
headlines.description: The heatwave is expected to spread to other parts of western Europe on Wednesday, before extending eastwards over the weekend.

headlines.title: Want to cool down in the water? Here's how to do it safely
headlines.description: Pick designated swimming spots, learn about riptides and don't use inflatables at the beach, experts say.

headlines.title: No need to panic for England - but this was a reality check
headlines.description: England's drab goalless draw with Ghana is no cause to panic - but it does serve as a reality check, writes Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: Silly tackle, bad reaction - Tuchel defends Bellingham after Queiroz row
headlines.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel defends Jude Bellingham after the midfielder is involved in a heated row with Ghana boss Carlos Queiroz.

headlines.title: Scotland primed for game of their lives against fallible Brazil
headlines.description: Scotland don't know what they need against Brazil to reach the World Cup knockout phase, but will know they need to improve on the previous two games, writes Tom English.

headlines.title: The economic challenges facing the next prime minister
headlines.description: Though the person in charge of the country will change, the fiscal issues remain the same.

headlines.title: Stanford was their golden ticket - could AI help or hinder that?
headlines.description: The BBC spoke with Stanford University graduates about what they really think about artificial intelligence.

headlines.title: How Washington DC's Reflecting Pool became a global talking point
headlines.description: President Trump says the pool is probably going to be drained again, two weeks after a multi-million dollar renovation was completed.

headlines.title: Luxury watches and £220 teapot: Police pictures reveal Peter Murrell's illicit purchases
headlines.description: Dozens of new images released by the Crown Office and Procurator Fiscal Service now show some of the items he purchased illegally.

headlines.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
headlines.description: The resolution passed on Tuesday was largely symbolic, but it adds to pressure on the White House to end the conflict once and for all.

headlines.title: Jesy Nelson 'outraged' after MPs debate muscle condition that affects her twins
headlines.description: The singer says children face a "postcode lottery", as testing for the disease is staggered.

headlines.description: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

headlines.title: Bondi Beach shooting hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on his father
headlines.description: Ahmed al Ahmed tackled one of the gunmen who killed 15 people at a Jewish event in December.

headlines.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
headlines.description: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

headlines.title: Lazio to pay compensation in pregnancy dispute case
